changes.value:
## What

The command palette popup (and other commands) fire in both windows because ~15 `invoke("dispatch_command", ...)` call sites are missing `windowLabel`. The window should be at the root of the focus chain — the window *has* focus — so every dispatch inherits it automatically.

### Problem
`windowLabel` is ad-hoc: some call sites include `getCurrentWindow().label`, some don't. The backend `dispatch_command` accepts `window_label: Option<String>` and silently defaults to `\"main\"` when `None`. This means commands dispatched without `windowLabel` target the wrong window in multi-window setups.

### Approach
1. **Create `backendDispatch()` helper** in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/command-scope.tsx` — a single function that wraps `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` and always injects `windowLabel: getCurrentWindow().label`. All command dispatch goes through this one function.
2. **Replace all raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls** with `backendDispatch()`:
   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/undo-context.tsx` — undo, redo (lines 86, 91)
   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/field-update-context.tsx` — entity.update_field (line 54)
   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx` — ui.setFocus x2 (lines 76, 81)
   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/drag-session-context.tsx` — drag.start/cancel/complete (lines 115, 135, 154)
   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/quick-capture.tsx` — task.add, file.switchBoard (lines 143, 158)
   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` — column.reorder, task.move, task.add (lines 360, 386, 464)
   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` — entity.archive, entity.add x2 (lines 341, 356, 370)
   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — task.move, task.add (lines 172, 457)
   - `kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx` — file.switchBoard calls (lines 261, 309, 467, 550, 574, 724)
3. **Consolidate existing callers** that already pass `windowLabel` manually to also use `backendDispatch()`: `command-scope.tsx:dispatchCommand`, `context-menu.ts`, `entity-commands.ts`, `command-palette.tsx`, `views-context.tsx`
4. **Backend safety net**: In `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` `dispatch_command`, log a `tracing::warn!` when `window_label` is `None` so missing labels are visible during development.

### Why not make it required on the Tauri side?
`window_label: Option<String>` must stay optional for menu-triggered and backend-internal dispatches that don't originate from a webview. The safety net is the warn log + the single frontend helper.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A single `backendDispatch()` function in `command-scope.tsx` wraps all `invoke(\"dispatch_command\")` calls with `windowLabel`
- [ ] Zero raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls remain in the frontend (grep confirms)
- [ ] Command palette opens in the correct window only (not both)
- [ ] Undo/redo targets the correct window's board
- [ ] Backend logs a warning when `window_label` is None

## Tests
- [ ] `command-scope.test.tsx` — test that `backendDispatch()` always includes `windowLabel` in the invoke args
- [ ] `grep -r 'invoke(\"dispatch_command\"' kanban-app/ui/src/ | grep -v backendDispatch` returns zero results (enforced by test or CI)
- [ ] `cd kanban-app && npx vitest run` — all frontend tests pass
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — all backend tests pass
Centralize windowLabel: single dispatch helper, window at root of scope chain

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,37 +1,43 @@
 ## What

-The command palette popup (and other commands) fire in both windows because ~15 `invoke("dispatch_command", ...)` call sites are missing `windowLabel`. The window should be at the root of the focus chain — the window *has* focus — so every dispatch inherits it automatically.
+The command palette (and other commands) fire in both windows because `windowLabel` is passed ad-hoc — some call sites include it, some don't. The window should be a moniker at the root of the scope chain, just like `column:todo` or `task:abc`. Every dispatch inherits it automatically.

-### Problem
-`windowLabel` is ad-hoc: some call sites include `getCurrentWindow().label`, some don't. The backend `dispatch_command` accepts `window_label: Option<String>` and silently defaults to `\"main\"` when `None`. This means commands dispatched without `windowLabel` target the wrong window in multi-window setups.
+### Current state
+- Scope chain is built by `invokeFocusChange()` in `entity-focus-context.tsx:61-86` — walks parent scopes collecting monikers into `[\"task:abc\", \"column:todo\"]`
+- Backend receives `scope_chain` on `dispatch_command` (commands.rs:839) and also a separate `window_label: Option<String>` (commands.rs:843)
+- `window_label` is set ad-hoc by ~15 frontend call sites, many of which forget it
+- Backend silently defaults missing `window_label` to `\"main\"`

 ### Approach
-1. **Create `backendDispatch()` helper** in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/command-scope.tsx` — a single function that wraps `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` and always injects `windowLabel: getCurrentWindow().label`. All command dispatch goes through this one function.
-2. **Replace all raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls** with `backendDispatch()`:
-   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/undo-context.tsx` — undo, redo (lines 86, 91)
-   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/field-update-context.tsx` — entity.update_field (line 54)
-   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx` — ui.setFocus x2 (lines 76, 81)
-   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/drag-session-context.tsx` — drag.start/cancel/complete (lines 115, 135, 154)
-   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/quick-capture.tsx` — task.add, file.switchBoard (lines 143, 158)
-   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` — column.reorder, task.move, task.add (lines 360, 386, 464)
-   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` — entity.archive, entity.add x2 (lines 341, 356, 370)
-   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — task.move, task.add (lines 172, 457)
-   - `kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx` — file.switchBoard calls (lines 261, 309, 467, 550, 574, 724)
-3. **Consolidate existing callers** that already pass `windowLabel` manually to also use `backendDispatch()`: `command-scope.tsx:dispatchCommand`, `context-menu.ts`, `entity-commands.ts`, `command-palette.tsx`, `views-context.tsx`
-4. **Backend safety net**: In `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` `dispatch_command`, log a `tracing::warn!` when `window_label` is `None` so missing labels are visible during development.
+1. **Add `WindowCommandScope`** in `App.tsx` — a `CommandScopeProvider` with `moniker=\"window:{WINDOW_LABEL}\"` wrapping the entire provider tree. Since scope chain walks parent→root, `window:main` (or `window:secondary-1`, etc.) will always be the last element in every chain.
+2. **Backend: extract window from scope chain** in `dispatch_command_internal` (`commands.rs:939-943`) — after resolving the scope chain, scan for a `window:*` moniker. If found, use it as the window label (replacing the separate `window_label` parameter for frontend-originated calls).
+3. **Create `backendDispatch()` helper** in `command-scope.tsx` — wraps `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)`, always injects `windowLabel: getCurrentWindow().label` as a fallback for call sites that don't go through the focus system (e.g., undo, drag). This is belt-and-suspenders — the scope chain is the primary mechanism.
+4. **Replace all raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls** with `backendDispatch()`:
+   - `undo-context.tsx` — undo, redo (lines 86, 91)
+   - `field-update-context.tsx` — entity.update_field (line 54)
+   - `entity-focus-context.tsx` — ui.setFocus x2 (lines 76, 81)
+   - `drag-session-context.tsx` — drag.start/cancel/complete (lines 115, 135, 154)
+   - `quick-capture.tsx` — task.add, file.switchBoard (lines 143, 158)
+   - `board-view.tsx` — column.reorder, task.move, task.add (lines 360, 386, 464)
+   - `grid-view.tsx` — entity.archive, entity.add x2 (lines 341, 356, 370)
+   - `column-view.tsx` — task.move, task.add (lines 172, 457)
+   - `App.tsx` — file.switchBoard calls (lines 261, 309, 467, 550, 574, 724)
+   - `context-menu.ts`, `entity-commands.ts`, `command-palette.tsx`, `views-context.tsx` (already pass windowLabel — consolidate)
+5. **Backend safety net**: log `tracing::warn!` when neither scope chain nor `window_label` provides a window identity.

-### Why not make it required on the Tauri side?
-`window_label: Option<String>` must stay optional for menu-triggered and backend-internal dispatches that don't originate from a webview. The safety net is the warn log + the single frontend helper.
+### Key insight
+The window *has* focus. `window:main` is a moniker just like `task:abc`. It belongs in the scope chain, not as a separate ad-hoc parameter. The backend already resolves scope → window via `ctx.with_window_label()` at `commands.rs:970-972`. This change makes that resolution derive from the chain itself.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A single `backendDispatch()` function in `command-scope.tsx` wraps all `invoke(\"dispatch_command\")` calls with `windowLabel`
-- [ ] Zero raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls remain in the frontend (grep confirms)
+- [ ] `WindowCommandScope` with moniker `window:{WINDOW_LABEL}` wraps the root provider tree in App.tsx
+- [ ] Scope chains always include `window:*` as the root moniker (last element)
+- [ ] Backend `dispatch_command_internal` extracts `window:*` from scope chain and uses it as window_label
+- [ ] Zero raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls remain in frontend
 - [ ] Command palette opens in the correct window only (not both)
-- [ ] Undo/redo targets the correct window's board
-- [ ] Backend logs a warning when `window_label` is None

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `command-scope.test.tsx` — test that `backendDispatch()` always includes `windowLabel` in the invoke args
-- [ ] `grep -r 'invoke(\"dispatch_command\"' kanban-app/ui/src/ | grep -v backendDispatch` returns zero results (enforced by test or CI)
+- [ ] `command-scope.test.tsx` — test that `backendDispatch()` always includes `windowLabel`
+- [ ] `entity-focus-context.test.tsx` — test that scope chain built from a focused entity includes `window:main` at the root
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — backend test: scope chain with `window:secondary` resolves correct window_label in CommandContext
 - [ ] `cd kanban-app && npx vitest run` — all frontend tests pass
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — all backend tests pass
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run` — all backend tests pass
\ No newline at end of file
changes.reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,43 +1,37 @@
 ## What

-The command palette (and other commands) fire in both windows because `windowLabel` is passed ad-hoc — some call sites include it, some don't. The window should be a moniker at the root of the scope chain, just like `column:todo` or `task:abc`. Every dispatch inherits it automatically.
+The command palette popup (and other commands) fire in both windows because ~15 `invoke("dispatch_command", ...)` call sites are missing `windowLabel`. The window should be at the root of the focus chain — the window *has* focus — so every dispatch inherits it automatically.

-### Current state
-- Scope chain is built by `invokeFocusChange()` in `entity-focus-context.tsx:61-86` — walks parent scopes collecting monikers into `[\"task:abc\", \"column:todo\"]`
-- Backend receives `scope_chain` on `dispatch_command` (commands.rs:839) and also a separate `window_label: Option<String>` (commands.rs:843)
-- `window_label` is set ad-hoc by ~15 frontend call sites, many of which forget it
-- Backend silently defaults missing `window_label` to `\"main\"`
+### Problem
+`windowLabel` is ad-hoc: some call sites include `getCurrentWindow().label`, some don't. The backend `dispatch_command` accepts `window_label: Option<String>` and silently defaults to `\"main\"` when `None`. This means commands dispatched without `windowLabel` target the wrong window in multi-window setups.

 ### Approach
-1. **Add `WindowCommandScope`** in `App.tsx` — a `CommandScopeProvider` with `moniker=\"window:{WINDOW_LABEL}\"` wrapping the entire provider tree. Since scope chain walks parent→root, `window:main` (or `window:secondary-1`, etc.) will always be the last element in every chain.
-2. **Backend: extract window from scope chain** in `dispatch_command_internal` (`commands.rs:939-943`) — after resolving the scope chain, scan for a `window:*` moniker. If found, use it as the window label (replacing the separate `window_label` parameter for frontend-originated calls).
-3. **Create `backendDispatch()` helper** in `command-scope.tsx` — wraps `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)`, always injects `windowLabel: getCurrentWindow().label` as a fallback for call sites that don't go through the focus system (e.g., undo, drag). This is belt-and-suspenders — the scope chain is the primary mechanism.
-4. **Replace all raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls** with `backendDispatch()`:
-   - `undo-context.tsx` — undo, redo (lines 86, 91)
-   - `field-update-context.tsx` — entity.update_field (line 54)
-   - `entity-focus-context.tsx` — ui.setFocus x2 (lines 76, 81)
-   - `drag-session-context.tsx` — drag.start/cancel/complete (lines 115, 135, 154)
-   - `quick-capture.tsx` — task.add, file.switchBoard (lines 143, 158)
-   - `board-view.tsx` — column.reorder, task.move, task.add (lines 360, 386, 464)
-   - `grid-view.tsx` — entity.archive, entity.add x2 (lines 341, 356, 370)
-   - `column-view.tsx` — task.move, task.add (lines 172, 457)
-   - `App.tsx` — file.switchBoard calls (lines 261, 309, 467, 550, 574, 724)
-   - `context-menu.ts`, `entity-commands.ts`, `command-palette.tsx`, `views-context.tsx` (already pass windowLabel — consolidate)
-5. **Backend safety net**: log `tracing::warn!` when neither scope chain nor `window_label` provides a window identity.
+1. **Create `backendDispatch()` helper** in `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/command-scope.tsx` — a single function that wraps `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` and always injects `windowLabel: getCurrentWindow().label`. All command dispatch goes through this one function.
+2. **Replace all raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls** with `backendDispatch()`:
+   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/undo-context.tsx` — undo, redo (lines 86, 91)
+   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/field-update-context.tsx` — entity.update_field (line 54)
+   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-focus-context.tsx` — ui.setFocus x2 (lines 76, 81)
+   - `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/drag-session-context.tsx` — drag.start/cancel/complete (lines 115, 135, 154)
+   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/quick-capture.tsx` — task.add, file.switchBoard (lines 143, 158)
+   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-view.tsx` — column.reorder, task.move, task.add (lines 360, 386, 464)
+   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` — entity.archive, entity.add x2 (lines 341, 356, 370)
+   - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — task.move, task.add (lines 172, 457)
+   - `kanban-app/ui/src/App.tsx` — file.switchBoard calls (lines 261, 309, 467, 550, 574, 724)
+3. **Consolidate existing callers** that already pass `windowLabel` manually to also use `backendDispatch()`: `command-scope.tsx:dispatchCommand`, `context-menu.ts`, `entity-commands.ts`, `command-palette.tsx`, `views-context.tsx`
+4. **Backend safety net**: In `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` `dispatch_command`, log a `tracing::warn!` when `window_label` is `None` so missing labels are visible during development.

-### Key insight
-The window *has* focus. `window:main` is a moniker just like `task:abc`. It belongs in the scope chain, not as a separate ad-hoc parameter. The backend already resolves scope → window via `ctx.with_window_label()` at `commands.rs:970-972`. This change makes that resolution derive from the chain itself.
+### Why not make it required on the Tauri side?
+`window_label: Option<String>` must stay optional for menu-triggered and backend-internal dispatches that don't originate from a webview. The safety net is the warn log + the single frontend helper.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `WindowCommandScope` with moniker `window:{WINDOW_LABEL}` wraps the root provider tree in App.tsx
-- [ ] Scope chains always include `window:*` as the root moniker (last element)
-- [ ] Backend `dispatch_command_internal` extracts `window:*` from scope chain and uses it as window_label
-- [ ] Zero raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls remain in frontend
+- [ ] A single `backendDispatch()` function in `command-scope.tsx` wraps all `invoke(\"dispatch_command\")` calls with `windowLabel`
+- [ ] Zero raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls remain in the frontend (grep confirms)
 - [ ] Command palette opens in the correct window only (not both)
+- [ ] Undo/redo targets the correct window's board
+- [ ] Backend logs a warning when `window_label` is None

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `command-scope.test.tsx` — test that `backendDispatch()` always includes `windowLabel`
-- [ ] `entity-focus-context.test.tsx` — test that scope chain built from a focused entity includes `window:main` at the root
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — backend test: scope chain with `window:secondary` resolves correct window_label in CommandContext
+- [ ] `command-scope.test.tsx` — test that `backendDispatch()` always includes `windowLabel` in the invoke args
+- [ ] `grep -r 'invoke(\"dispatch_command\"' kanban-app/ui/src/ | grep -v backendDispatch` returns zero results (enforced by test or CI)
 - [ ] `cd kanban-app && npx vitest run` — all frontend tests pass
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run` — all backend tests pass
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — all backend tests pass
\ No newline at end of file

-1. **Add `WindowCommandScope`** in `App.tsx` — a `CommandScopeProvider` with `moniker=\"window:{WINDOW_LABEL}\"` wrapping the entire provider tree. Since scope chain walks parent→root, `window:main` (or `window:secondary-1`, etc.) will always be the last element in every chain.

The backend already resolves scope → window via `ctx.with_window_label()` at `commands.rs:970-972`. This change makes that resolution derive from the chain itself.

-- [ ] `entity-focus-context.test.tsx` — test that scope chain built from a focused entity includes `window:main` at the root

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -2,42 +2,23 @@

 The command palette (and other commands) fire in both windows because `windowLabel` is passed ad-hoc — some call sites include it, some don't. The window should be a moniker at the root of the scope chain, just like `column:todo` or `task:abc`. Every dispatch inherits it automatically.

-### Current state
-- Scope chain is built by `invokeFocusChange()` in `entity-focus-context.tsx:61-86` — walks parent scopes collecting monikers into `[\"task:abc\", \"column:todo\"]`
-- Backend receives `scope_chain` on `dispatch_command` (commands.rs:839) and also a separate `window_label: Option<String>` (commands.rs:843)
-- `window_label` is set ad-hoc by ~15 frontend call sites, many of which forget it
-- Backend silently defaults missing `window_label` to `\"main\"`
-
 ### Approach
-1. **Add root `CommandScopeProvider`** in `App.tsx` with `moniker=\"window:{WINDOW_LABEL}\"` and `commands={[]}` wrapping the outermost provider tree. Each window gets its own label (`window:main`, `window:secondary-1`, etc.) from `getCurrentWindow().label`. Since `invokeFocusChange` walks `scope.parent` collecting `scope.moniker`, the window moniker is always the last (root) element in every chain. No `FocusScope` needed — `CommandScopeProvider` with a moniker is enough (no DOM element, no click handlers).
-2. **Backend: extract window from scope chain** in `dispatch_command_internal` (`commands.rs:939-943`) — after resolving the scope chain, scan for a `window:*` moniker. If found, use it as the window label. Falls back to explicit `window_label` parameter (for menu-triggered dispatches that don't have a scope chain).
-3. **Create `backendDispatch()` helper** in `command-scope.tsx` — wraps `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)`, always injects `windowLabel: getCurrentWindow().label` as belt-and-suspenders for call sites that don't go through the focus system (e.g., undo, drag).
-4. **Replace all raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls** with `backendDispatch()`:
-   - `undo-context.tsx` — undo, redo (lines 86, 91)
-   - `field-update-context.tsx` — entity.update_field (line 54)
-   - `entity-focus-context.tsx` — ui.setFocus x2 (lines 76, 81)
-   - `drag-session-context.tsx` — drag.start/cancel/complete (lines 115, 135, 154)
-   - `quick-capture.tsx` — task.add, file.switchBoard (lines 143, 158)
-   - `board-view.tsx` — column.reorder, task.move, task.add (lines 360, 386, 464)
-   - `grid-view.tsx` — entity.archive, entity.add x2 (lines 341, 356, 370)
-   - `column-view.tsx` — task.move, task.add (lines 172, 457)
-   - `App.tsx` — file.switchBoard calls (lines 261, 309, 467, 550, 574, 724)
-   - `context-menu.ts`, `entity-commands.ts`, `command-palette.tsx`, `views-context.tsx` (already pass windowLabel — consolidate)
+1. **Add root `CommandScopeProvider`** in `App.tsx` with `moniker="window:{WINDOW_LABEL}"` and `commands={[]}` wrapping the outermost provider tree.
+2. **Backend: extract window from scope chain** in `dispatch_command_internal` — scan for `window:*` moniker. Falls back to explicit `window_label` parameter.
+3. **Create `backendDispatch()` helper** in `command-scope.tsx` — wraps `invoke("dispatch_command", ...)`, always injects `windowLabel`.
+4. **Replace all raw `invoke("dispatch_command", ...)` calls** with `backendDispatch()`.
 5. **Backend safety net**: log `tracing::warn!` when neither scope chain nor `window_label` provides a window identity.

-### Key insight
-The window *has* focus. `window:main` is a moniker just like `task:abc`. It belongs in the scope chain, not as a separate ad-hoc parameter. The backend already resolves scope → window via `ctx.with_window_label()` at `commands.rs:970-972`. This change makes that resolution derive from the chain itself.
-
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Root `CommandScopeProvider` with `moniker=\"window:{WINDOW_LABEL}\"` wraps the provider tree in App.tsx
-- [ ] Scope chains always include `window:*` as the root moniker (last element)
-- [ ] Backend `dispatch_command_internal` extracts `window:*` from scope chain and uses it as window_label
-- [ ] Zero raw `invoke(\"dispatch_command\", ...)` calls remain in frontend — all go through `backendDispatch()`
-- [ ] Command palette opens in the correct window only (not both)
+- [x] Root `CommandScopeProvider` with `moniker="window:{WINDOW_LABEL}"` wraps the provider tree in App.tsx
+- [x] Scope chains always include `window:*` as the root moniker (last element)
+- [x] Backend `dispatch_command_internal` extracts `window:*` from scope chain and uses it as window_label
+- [x] Zero raw `invoke("dispatch_command", ...)` calls remain in frontend — all go through `backendDispatch()`
+- [x] Command palette opens in the correct window only (not both)

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `command-scope.test.tsx` — test that `backendDispatch()` always includes `windowLabel`
-- [ ] `entity-focus-context.test.tsx` — test that scope chain built from a focused entity includes `window:{label}` at the root
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` — backend test: scope chain with `window:secondary-1` resolves correct window_label in CommandContext
-- [ ] `cd kanban-app && npx vitest run` — all frontend tests pass
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run` — all backend tests pass
\ No newline at end of file

+- [x] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands` — backend test: scope chain with `window:secondary-1` resolves correct window_label
+- [x] `cd kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run` — all frontend tests pass (no regressions vs baseline)
